rules: Prefer primary sources, court records, official releases, archives, and dated reporting.
Do not treat a news mention as proof of wrongdoing.
Every item must keep a source URL, source label, date, lane, and reader route.
Every update should connect to evidence, video, opt-in, offer, and book/store routes where relevant.
Every update should give the reader a next action: source, evidence, video, free brief, offer, or book.

items.title: Epstein Evidence Watch activated
items.summary: The Epstein lane is configured for source-watch updates, document routes, dated bulletins, video routes, opt-ins, offers, and book/store pathways.
items.evidenceBoundary: A public-record association is not proof of wrongdoing. Follow the source route before sharing the claim.
items.whyItMatters: This turns a high-interest public-record topic into a structured route: source first, evidence boundary second, reader action third.
items.nextAction: Open the Epstein Files hub, check the source lanes, then route the reader into the free brief, video channel, and Black File book path.
items.videoHook: The files are not useful because they are famous. They are useful when every name, document, and claim is tied to a source boundary.
items.rumbleShortTitle: The Epstein Files Problem Nobody Explains
items.rumbleLongTitle: How To Read Epstein Files Without Losing The Evidence Boundary
socialThread: 1/ The Epstein files are not a meme. They are a public-record maze.
2/ A name in a document is not proof of a crime. The source type matters.
3/ Court record, archive, news claim, commentary, and allegation are different evidence levels.
4/ Matrix Reprogrammed now routes each update into source lanes, video hooks, free briefs, and book paths.
